name: The Quiet Luxurist
description: Knows exactly what they want and it's expensive, but they would never say so. High Design, high Service, but zero interest in spectacle. The opposite of the Instagram Aesthete — same budget, completely different values. Aman, not Nobu. Shaker furniture, not statement pieces. Tests whether the system can distinguish quiet luxury from loud luxury across the same domain peaks.

contradictions.stated: Doesn't care about luxury — just wants things done well
contradictions.revealed: Actually spends $800+ a night routinely. The 'I don't care about luxury' framing is itself a luxury position — they've so internalized high standards that they've stopped registering them as luxury
contradictions.expectedResolution: Ultra-premium properties with understated presentation — Aman, Benesse House, quality over brand

voiceAnswerGuidance.memorable-stays: Short, precise, authoritative. 'Aman Tokyo. Benesse House in Naoshima. La Colombe d'Or.' Doesn't explain why — expects you to understand. If pressed: 'Everything was considered. Nothing was wasted. You could feel the intention in every surface.'
voiceAnswerGuidance.anti-stay: One word could suffice. 'Vegas.' If pressed: 'Anything that confuses volume with quality. Gold taps. Marble lobbies. A concierge who calls you by name too eagerly.'
voiceAnswerGuidance.go-back-place: Somewhere specific and unexpected. 'A small hotel in the Iya Valley, Shikoku. Run by an architect. Eight rooms. The most extraordinary use of cypress I've ever seen.'
voiceAnswerGuidance.cultural-engagement: Quiet, private, connoisseurial. Gallery visits arranged by the owner. A private tea ceremony. 'I don't want the tourist version of anything.'
voiceAnswerGuidance.emotional-core: Travels to encounter quality. Not escape, not adventure — refinement. 'I want to be in a space where someone has thought deeply about every decision.' CONNOISSEUR.
